title: The NDPA Notification Letter
description:
Draft the letter Sankofa Digital will file with the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC) under Section 40 of the NDPA 2023. You have 72 hours from the moment of discovery — the clock started at 04:12 UTC on 2024-01-15.

The letter must:

  •  State the data controller's name and the DPO's name+contact (you may invent reasonable placeholders).
  •  State the categories and approximate volume of affected data subjects (base this on what Stage 2 revealed about the exposed Elasticsearch index — do not inflate).
  •  State the nature of the breach in plain language (three sentences max).
  •  State the consequences and the containment actions already taken.
  •  State the remediation roadmap.
  •  Sign off in a form that would be defensible if quoted in court.

Avoid: unnecessary hedging, legalese you cannot define, and figures you cannot support with evidence from the investigation. A letter that misstates facts is worse than a late letter.

This is a board-visible artefact — it will be quoted back at you if the investigation concludes differently. Write it accordingly.

widgetConfig.placeholder:
Nigeria Data Protection Commission
No. 10 Broad Street, Central Business District
Abuja

15 January 2024

Re: Notification of Personal Data Breach — Sankofa Digital Ltd

Dear Commissioner, ...
